title: Wheat Flours Guide
content:
WHEAT FLOURS

All-Purpose Flour: Blend of hard and soft wheats. Higher gluten content gives elasticity. Unbleached preferred for nutritional value. Standard substitution: 1⅞ cups cake flour = 1 cup all-purpose.

Cake Flour: Made from soft wheats; delicate, less expansive gluten. For tender, crumbly texture. Substitution: 1 cup minus 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour.

Bread Flour: High gluten/protein content. Gives elasticity and better rise. Feels granular when rubbed between fingers.

Whole Wheat/Graham Flour: Contains bran and germ. Higher nutritional value. Substitute 1 cup fine whole wheat for 1 cup all-purpose; 1 cup plus 2 tablespoons coarse whole wheat.

Pastry Flour: Soft, finely milled, low-gluten. Good for quick breads and pastries.

Self-Rising/Phosphated: Contains leavens and salt. Use only as specified; leavens may lose potency with storage.

Gluten Flour: Starch-free, high-protein. Substitute 13 tablespoons gluten flour for 1 cup all-purpose.
